title: Extract dS Lichnerowicz eigenvalue shifts c_J from PSALTer paper

description: Implement parametrized post-Newtonian (PPN) formalism in TensorGR.jl. PPN metric ansatz with 10 parameters (γ, β, ξ, α₁, α₂, α₃, ζ₁, ζ₂, ζ₃, ζ₄), velocity-order expansion, PPN potentials, and order-by-order field equation solving. Validate against GR and Brans-Dicke. Ground truth: Will, 'Theory and Experiment in Gravitational Physics' (2018); Will, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 4 (2014). New directory: src/ppn/.
title: Parametrized Post-Newtonian Formalism (xPPN)

description:
Design the parametrized post-Newtonian framework. Key decisions:
- How to implement velocity-order expansion (v/c powers) within existing perturbation engine
- 10 PPN parameters: γ, β, ξ, α₁, α₂, α₃, ζ₁, ζ₂, ζ₃, ζ₄
- Integration with existing pn_order/truncate_pn in src/worldline/
- PPN potentials as abstract tensors vs component expressions
- API: define_ppn_metric!, ppn_params, expand_ppn, ppn_eos
- New directory: src/ppn/

Ground truth: Will, 'Theory and Experiment in Gravitational Physics' (2018), Ch 4; Will, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 4 (2014).

Acceptance: Design document specifying type hierarchy, API surface, integration points with existing code.
title: Research: Design PPN framework for TensorGR.jl

description:
Implement the standard PPN metric ansatz:
- g₀₀ = -1 + 2U - 2βU² + 2ξΦ_W + (2γ+2+α₃+ζ₁-2ξ)Φ₁ + 2(3γ-2β+1+ζ₂+ξ)Φ₂ + 2(1+ζ₃)Φ₃ + 2(3γ+3ζ₄-2ξ)Φ₄ - (ζ₁-2ξ)𝒜
- g₀ᵢ = -½(4γ+3+α₁-α₂+ζ₁-2ξ)Vᵢ - ½(1+α₂-ζ₁+2ξ)Wᵢ
- gᵢⱼ = (1+2γU)δᵢⱼ

Build as abstract tensor expressions with PPN parameters as symbolic scalars. Add to src/ppn/.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Eqs 4.1-4.3.

Acceptance: ppn_metric(params) returns (g00, g0i, gij) as TensorExpr. Parameters appear symbolically.
title: xPPN: Implement PPN metric ansatz

description:
Implement PPNParams struct holding all 10 PPN parameters with default GR values:
- γ = 1 (space curvature per unit mass)
- β = 1 (nonlinearity in gravity)
- ξ = 0 (preferred-location effects)
- α₁ = α₂ = α₃ = 0 (preferred-frame effects)
- ζ₁ = ζ₂ = ζ₃ = ζ₄ = 0 (conservation law violation)

Include named constructors for common theories: PPNParams(:GR), PPNParams(:BransDicke, omega=ω). Add to src/ppn/.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Table 4.1.

Acceptance: PPNParams() gives GR defaults. is_fully_conservative(p) checks ζ's = 0. is_preferred_frame_free(p) checks α's = 0.
title: xPPN: Implement PPNParams type with GR defaults

description:
Implement velocity-order (v/c) expansion for tensors. Extend existing pn_order/truncate_pn from src/worldline/. Key:
- Track v/c order of each quantity: U ~ O(2), v^i ~ O(1), p ~ O(2), etc.
- Expand metric, Christoffel, Ricci to required order
- Truncate at specified order: truncate_ppn(expr, order)
- g₀₀ needs through O(4), g₀ᵢ through O(3), gᵢⱼ through O(2)

Add to src/ppn/.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Ch 4 (order counting); existing pn_order/truncate_pn API.

Acceptance: ppn_order(expr) returns v/c order. truncate_ppn(expr, n) drops terms above order n. Consistent with existing worldline PN infrastructure.
title: xPPN: Implement velocity-order expansion

description:
Register PPN potential tensors with appropriate properties:
- U: Newtonian potential (scalar, O(2))
- Uᵢⱼ: super-potential (symmetric rank-2 spatial, O(2))
- Φ_W: Whitehead potential (scalar, O(4))
- Φ₁, Φ₂, Φ₃, Φ₄: post-Newtonian potentials (scalar, O(4))
- 𝒜: preferred-frame potential (scalar, O(4))
- Vᵢ, Wᵢ: vector potentials (spatial vector, O(3))

Each defined with integral representations (Poisson-type integrals over matter source). Add to src/ppn/.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Eqs 4.4-4.12.

Acceptance: All potentials registered with correct v/c order, symmetry. Integral representations stored as metadata.
title: xPPN: Implement PPN potentials (U, Uᵢⱼ, Φ_W, Φ₁-Φ₄, 𝒜, Vᵢ, Wᵢ)

description:
Implement order-by-order solving of field equations in the PPN framework. Given a gravitational theory (action or field equations), solve for the metric order-by-order in v/c:
1. At O(2): solve for g₀₀^(2) and gᵢⱼ^(2) in terms of U
2. At O(3): solve for g₀ᵢ^(3) in terms of Vᵢ, Wᵢ
3. At O(4): solve for g₀₀^(4) in terms of Φ's
4. Read off PPN parameters from coefficients

Use existing solve_tensors infrastructure. Add to src/ppn/.

Ground truth: Will (2018) Ch 5.

Acceptance: ppn_solve(field_eqs, matter_source; order=4) returns PPNParams. Correct for GR field equations.
title: xPPN: Implement PPN field equations (order-by-order solving)

description:
Validate PPN framework against Einstein's general relativity. Starting from EH action S = ∫R√(-g)d⁴x (or equivalently G_{μν} = 8πT_{μν}), solve for PPN parameters. Expected:
- γ = 1
- β = 1
- ξ = α₁ = α₂ = α₃ = 0
- ζ₁ = ζ₂ = ζ₃ = ζ₄ = 0

Ground truth: Will (2018) Table 4.1. STRING MATCH on all 10 parameters.

Acceptance: ppn_solve on GR field equations returns PPNParams matching all 10 GR values exactly.
title: xPPN Validation: GR PPN parameters (γ=β=1, rest=0)

description:
Validate PPN framework against Brans-Dicke theory. Action: S = ∫[φR - (ω/φ)(∂φ)² + L_m]√(-g)d⁴x. Solve for PPN parameters. Expected:
- γ = (1+ω)/(2+ω)
- β = 1
- All others = 0 (fully conservative, no preferred frame)

Note: γ → 1 as ω → ∞ (GR limit). Current solar system bound: ω > 40000 (Cassini).

Ground truth: Will (2018) Eq 5.23; Will, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 4 (2014). STRING MATCH on γ and β.

Acceptance: ppn_solve on Brans-Dicke field equations returns γ = (1+ω)/(2+ω), β = 1 symbolically.
title: xPPN Validation: Brans-Dicke PPN parameters

description: Validation: verify that the massive spin-2 mode in bimetric gravity on a de Sitter background satisfies the Higuchi bound m^2 >= 2 Lambda / 3. Below this bound, the helicity-0 mode of the massive graviton becomes a ghost (negative kinetic energy). Procedure: (1) set up bimetric theory on proportional dS backgrounds with cosmological constant Lambda, (2) compute the mass eigenstate mass m^2 from the beta parameters, (3) compute the kinetic matrix for all helicity components, (4) verify that the helicity-0 kinetic term flips sign when m^2 < 2 Lambda/3. Ground truth: Higuchi, Nucl. Phys. B 282, 397 (1987); Hassan, Schmidt-May & von Strauss, PLB 715, 335 (2012), arXiv:1203.5283. Test: STRING MATCH — at the Higuchi bound m^2 = 2 Lambda/3, the helicity-0 kinetic coefficient equals exactly zero, confirming the partially massless point. Coefficient is proportional to (m^2 - 2 Lambda/3).
title: Bimetric Validation: Verify Higuchi bound m^2 >= 2 Lambda/3 for massive spin-2 on de Sitter

description: Implement scalar-tensor gravity parametrization in new src/scalar_tensor/ directory. Horndeski theory with 4 free functions G_2..G_5(phi,X), Lagrangian construction, field equations, beyond-Horndeski (GLPV) extensions, Bellini-Sawicki alpha parametrization, quadratic action on FRW. Validation: f(R) as Horndeski subcase. Ground truth: Horndeski, Int. J. Theor. Phys. 10, 363 (1974); Kobayashi, Rep. Prog. Phys. 82, 086901 (2019), arXiv:1901.04778. ~100 LOC per issue.
title: xIST/COPPER Epic 6: Scalar-Tensor Theory

description: Research task: design types for Horndeski scalar-tensor theory in TensorGR.jl. Horndeski theory has 4 free functions G_2(phi, X), G_3(phi, X), G_4(phi, X), G_5(phi, X) where X = -(1/2)(nabla phi)^2. Key design decisions: (1) how to represent G_i as abstract functions of (phi, X) — options: symbolic functions, dispatch hooks, or new FunctionTensor type, (2) how phi interacts with existing tensor AST (scalar field as Tensor with 0 indices vs TScalar), (3) how X = -(1/2) g^{ab} nabla_a phi nabla_b phi is computed. Deliverable: design doc (~60 lines) with type hierarchy. Reference: Kobayashi, Rep. Prog. Phys. 82, 086901 (2019), Eq 2.1. Test: N/A (design only).
title: xIST: Research Horndeski theory parametrization design

description: Implement Horndeski Lagrangian density types. Four pieces: L_2 = G_2(phi,X), L_3 = -G_3(phi,X) Box(phi), L_4 = G_4(phi,X) R + G_{4X} [(Box phi)^2 - (nabla_a nabla_b phi)^2], L_5 = G_5(phi,X) G_{ab} nabla^a nabla^b phi - (1/6) G_{5X} [(Box phi)^3 - 3(Box phi)(nabla_a nabla_b phi)^2 + 2(nabla_a nabla_b phi)^3]. Implement HorndeskiLagrangian type storing 4 abstract functions and a scalar field. Provide horndeski_lagrangian(g, phi, G2, G3, G4, G5) constructor. File: src/scalar_tensor/horndeski.jl. Ground truth: Kobayashi Eqs 2.1-2.4. Test: GR limit G_4 = 1/(16 pi G), others = 0. ~120 LOC.
title: xIST: Implement Horndeski Lagrangian types L_2 through L_5

description: Implement the Horndeski field equations: (1) metric equation E_{ab} = 0, obtained by varying the total action S = integral(sqrt(-g) sum L_i) with respect to g^{ab}, and (2) scalar equation E_phi = 0, obtained by varying with respect to phi. These are second-order equations (Horndeski's key result: most general scalar-tensor theory with second-order EOMs in 4D). Provide horndeski_metric_eom(lagrangian) and horndeski_scalar_eom(lagrangian) returning TensorExpr. File: src/scalar_tensor/horndeski_eom.jl. Ground truth: Kobayashi Eqs 2.5-2.6. Test: GR limit gives Einstein equations. ~120 LOC.
title: xIST: Implement Horndeski field equations (metric + scalar EOMs)

description: Implement beyond-Horndeski extensions (Gleyzes-Langlois-Piazza-Vernizzi). Two additional Lagrangian pieces: L_4^{bH} = F_4(phi,X) epsilon^{abcd} epsilon^{efgd} nabla_a phi nabla_e phi nabla_b nabla_f phi nabla_c nabla_g phi, L_5^{bH} = F_5(phi,X) epsilon^{abcd} epsilon^{efgh} nabla_a phi nabla_e phi nabla_b nabla_f phi nabla_c nabla_g phi nabla_d nabla_h phi. These add 2 free functions F_4, F_5 beyond Horndeski. EOMs are higher-order but propagate only 3 DOF (metric + scalar). Extend HorndeskiLagrangian to BeyondHorndeskiLagrangian. File: src/scalar_tensor/beyond_horndeski.jl. Ground truth: Gleyzes et al, PRL 114, 211101 (2015). Test: F_4 = F_5 = 0 recovers Horndeski. ~100 LOC.
title: xIST: Implement beyond-Horndeski (GLPV) extensions

description: Implement the Bellini-Sawicki parametrization of linear cosmological perturbations in Horndeski theory. Four time-dependent functions on FRW background: alpha_M (running Planck mass), alpha_K (kineticity), alpha_B (braiding), alpha_T (tensor speed excess). Relations: alpha_M = d ln M*^2 / d ln a, alpha_K from G_2X etc., alpha_B from G_3 and G_4phi, alpha_T from G_4X and G_5. Provide compute_alphas(lagrangian, background) -> (alpha_M, alpha_K, alpha_B, alpha_T). File: src/scalar_tensor/alpha_params.jl. Ground truth: Bellini & Sawicki, JCAP 1407 (2014) 050; Kobayashi Sec 5.1. Test: GR gives all alphas = 0. ~100 LOC.
title: xIST: Implement Bellini-Sawicki alpha parametrization

description: Implement the quadratic action for cosmological perturbations in Horndeski theory on an FRW background, expressed in terms of the Bellini-Sawicki alpha parameters. The action splits into tensor and scalar sectors: S^{(2)}_T = integral(M*^2/8)[h_ij''^2 - (1+alpha_T) c^2 (nabla h_ij)^2], S^{(2)}_S = integral[Q_S zeta'^2 - Q_S c_s^2 (nabla zeta)^2] where Q_S and c_s^2 are combinations of alphas. Ghost-free: Q_S > 0. Gradient stability: c_s^2 > 0. Provide scalar_tensor_quadratic_action(alphas, background) using existing QuadraticForm infrastructure. File: src/scalar_tensor/quadratic_action_st.jl. Ground truth: Kobayashi Sec 5.2; Bellini & Sawicki Sec 3. Test: verify GR limit gives standard GW action with c_T = 1. ~120 LOC.
title: xIST: Implement quadratic action for scalar-tensor perturbations on FRW

description: Validation task: verify that f(R) gravity maps correctly to a Horndeski subcase. The mapping is: phi = f'(R), X = 0 (no kinetic term for phi), G_2 = f(R) - R f'(R) (potential), G_3 = 0, G_4 = f'(R)/2, G_5 = 0. This means f(R) is a scalar-tensor theory with no kinetic braiding and a specific potential. Verify: (1) construct Horndeski Lagrangian with these G_i, (2) field equations reproduce the f(R) trace equation Box f' - (1/3)(2f - f'R) = (1/3)T, (3) alpha parameters: alpha_T = 0 (gravitons travel at speed of light), alpha_B = -alpha_M/2, alpha_K depends on f''. File: test/scalar_tensor/test_fR_validation.jl. Ground truth: Kobayashi Sec 2.3. Test: all 3 checks pass. ~80 LOC.
title: xIST: Validation — f(R) gravity as Horndeski subcase

description: Derive Regge-Wheeler and Zerilli master equations from first principles using the harmonic infrastructure from Epics 1-3. Schwarzschild 2+2 decomposition, RW gauge, RW equation (odd-parity), Zerilli equation (even-parity), master function extraction. STRING MATCH validation against known potentials. Unblocks 3 existing @test_skip benchmarks. Ground truth: Regge & Wheeler, Phys. Rev. 108, 1063 (1957); Zerilli, Phys. Rev. D 2, 2141 (1970); Martel & Poisson Sec III-IV. ~100 LOC per issue.
title: Harmonics Epic 4: Regge-Wheeler / Zerilli Master Equations

description: Set up Schwarzschild background in 2+2 decomposition: ds^2 = -f(r)dt^2 + f(r)^{-1}dr^2 + r^2 d_Omega^2, where f(r) = 1 - 2M/r. Register the background metric as a 2D Lorentzian part (t,r) times S^2 with radius r. Use existing product_manifold infrastructure for M2 x S2 structure. Define tortoise coordinate r* via dr*/dr = 1/f(r). File: src/harmonics/schwarzschild.jl. Ground truth: Regge & Wheeler Sec 2; Martel & Poisson Sec III. Test: verify Schwarzschild Ricci = 0 (vacuum). ~100 LOC.
title: RW/Zerilli: Implement Schwarzschild background 2+2 decomposition

description: Implement the Regge-Wheeler gauge choice for odd-parity perturbations on Schwarzschild. Gauge conditions: h_{tr}^{odd} = h_{rr}^{odd} = h_{t}^{odd} = 0 (3 gauge freedoms used). This leaves a single odd-parity master variable. For even-parity, implement the RW gauge: h_{t,even}^a = 0, G_{lm} = 0 (removes 4 gauge freedoms). Implement as set_vanishing! rules applied after harmonic decomposition. File: src/harmonics/rw_gauge.jl. Ground truth: Regge & Wheeler Eq 6; Martel & Poisson Sec III.C. Test: apply gauge to generic perturbation, verify correct DOF count. ~80 LOC.
title: RW/Zerilli: Implement Regge-Wheeler gauge

description: Derive the Regge-Wheeler equation for odd-parity Schwarzschild perturbations: [-d^2/dt^2 + d^2/dr*^2 - V_RW(r)] psi_RW = 0, where V_RW(r) = (1 - 2M/r)[l(l+1)/r^2 - 6M/r^3]. Pipeline: (1) apply tensor harmonic decomposition to linearized Einstein equations, (2) apply RW gauge, (3) extract the single odd-parity master equation. STRING MATCH validation: the extracted potential must equal (1 - 2M/r)*(l*(l+1)/r^2 - 6*M/r^3). File: src/harmonics/regge_wheeler.jl. Ground truth: Regge & Wheeler Eq 11; Martel & Poisson Eq 4.9. Test: string match V_RW against known form. ~120 LOC.
title: RW/Zerilli: Derive Regge-Wheeler equation (odd-parity master)

description: Derive the Zerilli equation for even-parity Schwarzschild perturbations: [-d^2/dt^2 + d^2/dr*^2 - V_Z(r)] psi_Z = 0, where V_Z(r) = (1-2M/r)/(r^3 * (nr+3M)^2) * [2n^2(n+1)r^3 + 6n^2 M r^2 + 18nM^2 r + 18M^3] with n = (l-1)(l+2)/2. Pipeline: apply tensor harmonic decomposition to linearized Einstein, apply RW gauge for even sector, combine equations to single master variable psi_Z (Zerilli function). STRING MATCH validation against known V_Z. File: src/harmonics/zerilli.jl. Ground truth: Zerilli, Phys. Rev. D 2, 2141 (1970), Eq 11; Martel & Poisson Eq 4.22. ~120 LOC.
title: RW/Zerilli: Derive Zerilli equation (even-parity master)

description: Implement extraction of Regge-Wheeler and Zerilli master functions (psi_RW, psi_Z) from the metric perturbation h_{ab} in harmonic decomposition. For odd-parity: psi_RW = (r/l(l+1)) * f(r) * (d/dr(h_1/r) - (1/r) h_0) where h_0, h_1 are odd-parity RW gauge coefficients. For even-parity: psi_Z = r/(n+1) * [K + (f/(nr+3M))(r H_1 - r^2 dK/dr*)] where K, H_1 are even-parity coefficients. Provide extract_master_functions(h_decomposed) -> (psi_RW, psi_Z). File: src/harmonics/master_functions.jl. Ground truth: Martel & Poisson Eqs 4.7, 4.20. Test: extract from known perturbation. ~100 LOC.
title: RW/Zerilli: Implement master function extraction from metric perturbation

description: Validation task: verify that the Regge-Wheeler and Zerilli potentials are isospectral (produce the same quasinormal mode spectrum). This is a nontrivial result: V_RW and V_Z look completely different but are related by a Darboux/supersymmetric transformation. Verify: (1) V_RW and V_Z satisfy the partner potential relation V_{+/-} = W^2 +/- dW/dr* for some superpotential W(r*), (2) for l=2, M=1, evaluate both potentials numerically at r=3M,4M,6M,10M and verify they differ but share the same spectrum. This unblocks the 3 existing @test_skip benchmarks in benchmarks/. Ground truth: Chandrasekhar, Mathematical Theory of Black Holes, Ch 4; Martel & Poisson Sec V. Test: verify superpotential relation algebraically. ~80 LOC.
title: RW/Zerilli: Validation — isospectrality of V_RW and V_Z

description: Implement even-parity and odd-parity vector spherical harmonics on S^2. Even: Y^a_{lm} = D^a Y_{lm} (gradient type). Odd: X^a_{lm} = epsilon^a_b D^b Y_{lm}. Orthogonality relations and vector field decomposition. New files in src/harmonics/. Ground truth: Martel & Poisson, Phys. Rev. D 71, 104003 (2005), Sec II.C. ~100 LOC per issue.
title: Harmonics Epic 2: Vector Spherical Harmonics

description: Implement even-parity (polar) vector spherical harmonics: Y^a_{lm} = D^a Y_{lm}, where D^a is the covariant derivative on S^2. These are the gradient-type vector harmonics. New type EvenVectorHarmonic with (l, m) quantum numbers, l >= 1 (l=0 gives zero). Must carry a single S^2 index (angular). Properties: D_a Y^a_{lm} = -l(l+1) Y_{lm} (divergence). File: src/harmonics/vector_harmonics.jl. Ground truth: Martel & Poisson Eq 2.5. Test: verify divergence relation. ~100 LOC.
title: Harmonics: Implement even-parity vector harmonics Y^a_{lm}

description: Implement odd-parity (axial) vector spherical harmonics: X^a_{lm} = epsilon^a_b D^b Y_{lm}, where epsilon^a_b is the Levi-Civita tensor on S^2. These are the curl-type vector harmonics. New type OddVectorHarmonic with (l, m), l >= 1. Properties: D_a X^a_{lm} = 0 (divergence-free), epsilon^{ab} D_a X_{b,lm} = l(l+1) Y_{lm}. File: src/harmonics/vector_harmonics.jl (same file as even). Ground truth: Martel & Poisson Eq 2.6. Test: verify divergence-free property. ~80 LOC.
title: Harmonics: Implement odd-parity vector harmonics X^a_{lm}

description: Implement orthogonality integral relations for vector harmonics on S^2. Key relations: integral(Y^a_{lm} Y^*_{a,l'm'}) = l(l+1) delta_{ll'} delta_{mm'}, integral(X^a_{lm} X^*_{a,l'm'}) = l(l+1) delta_{ll'} delta_{mm'}, integral(Y^a_{lm} X^*_{a,l'm'}) = 0 (even-odd orthogonal). These become simplification rules for products integrated over S^2. File: src/harmonics/orthogonality.jl. Ground truth: Martel & Poisson Sec II.C. Test: verify all three orthogonality relations as RewriteRules. ~80 LOC.
title: Harmonics: Implement vector harmonic orthogonality relations

description: Implement decomposition of a vector field on S^2: V_a = sum_{l,m} [v^{even}_{lm}(t,r) Y^a_{lm} + v^{odd}_{lm}(t,r) X^a_{lm}]. Provide decompose_vector(V_expr, harmonics_config) that rewrites a vector TensorExpr carrying S^2 indices into a sum over (l,m) modes with even/odd radial coefficients. File: src/harmonics/decompose_vector.jl. Ground truth: Martel & Poisson Sec II.C. Test: decompose a generic vector V_a and verify even/odd mode separation. ~100 LOC.
title: Harmonics: Implement vector field harmonic decomposition

description: Implement linearized perturbation theory for Hassan-Rosen bimetric gravity. Perturb both metrics about proportional backgrounds: g_{ab} = g_bar_{ab} + delta_g_{ab}, f_{ab} = c^2 g_bar_{ab} + delta_f_{ab} where c is the background ratio. The linearized field equations couple delta_g and delta_f through the mass term derived from the Hassan-Rosen interaction potential V(g^{-1}f) = sum_{n=0}^{4} beta_n e_n(sqrt(g^{-1}f)). Express the coupled equations as a matrix system acting on (delta_g, delta_f). ~120 LOC in src/perturbation/bimetric.jl. Reference: Torsello et al, Class. Quant. Grav. 37, 025013 (2020), arXiv:1904.10464, Sec 4; Hassan & Rosen, JHEP 1202, 126 (2012), arXiv:1109.3515, Sec 3. Test: setting beta_0 = beta_1 = beta_3 = beta_4 = 0 (only beta_2 nonzero, i.e., Fierz-Pauli mass term) gives a single massive spin-2 equation for the massive combination, matching Fierz & Pauli, Proc. Roy. Soc. A 173, 211 (1939).
title: Bimetric: Implement linearized Hassan-Rosen perturbation equations for two coupled metrics

description: Implement the spinor Ricci identity (commutator of spin covariant derivatives): [nabla_{AA'}, nabla_{BB'}] phi_C = epsilon_{A'B'} Psi_{ABCD} phi^D + epsilon_{AB} Psi_bar_{A'B'C'D'} phi^{D'_equiv} + ... (curvature terms). Full formula involves Weyl spinor, Ricci spinor, and Lambda. Function: commute_spin_covds(expr; registry) -> expr with commutator replaced by curvature spinors. ~100 LOC in src/spinors/spin_commutator.jl. Reference: Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 (1984), Eqs 4.9.2-4.9.13. Test: (1) [nabla_{AA'}, nabla_{BB'}] phi_C string-matches P&R Eq 4.9.2, (2) vacuum (Phi=Lambda=0): commutator reduces to Weyl spinor terms only, (3) conformally flat (Psi=0): commutator reduces to Ricci terms.
title: Spinors: Implement spinor commutator identities

description: Implement automated contraction of Feynman diagrams: given a graph of propagators and vertices connected by internal lines, contract all internal Lorentz indices and sum over internal momenta (applying momentum conservation at each vertex). Must handle: (1) identify internal vs external index groups, (2) contract propagator D^{abcd} with vertex index slots, (3) produce scalar momentum integrals with correct numerator structure. Function: contract_diagram(diagram; registry) -> MomentumIntegral. ~120 LOC in src/feynman/contraction.jl. Reference: Goldberger & Rothstein, PRD 73, 104029 (2006), arXiv:hep-th/0409156, Sec 4-5. Test: tree-level 2-to-2 graviton scattering via s+t+u channel sum produces correct tensor structure, matching DeWitt vertex Feynman rules.
title: EFTofPNG: Implement tensor contraction engine for Feynman diagrams

description: Prerequisite infrastructure for fermion fields (FieldsX). Implement Clifford algebra representation for gamma matrices in TensorGR.jl: GammaMatrix type with {γ^a, γ^b} = 2g^{ab}, chirality matrix γ^5, trace identities, Fierz identities, charge conjugation. Ground truth: Wald GR Appendix B; Peskin & Schroeder Appendix A; Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 Ch 2. New directory: src/fermions/. ~700 LOC total across 7 sub-issues.
title: Gamma Matrices & Clifford Algebra

description: Design how to represent γ^a matrices in TensorGR.jl. Options: (A) abstract tensor with special contraction rules, (B) component matrices with symbolic entries. Must support: anticommutator {γ^a, γ^b} = 2g^{ab}, chirality γ^5 = iγ^0γ^1γ^2γ^3, trace identities Tr(γ^a γ^b) = 4g^{ab}. Key design decisions: (1) spinor index type (new VBundle or special annotation), (2) how gamma chain products simplify, (3) interaction with existing canonicalize pipeline. Deliverable: design document in issue notes. Ground truth: Wald GR Appendix B; Penrose & Rindler Vol 1 Ch 2. ~0 LOC (design only).
title: Research: Design Clifford algebra for TensorGR

description: Implement GammaMatrix type representing γ^a with a spacetime index. Core Clifford algebra contraction rule: {γ^a, γ^b} = 2g^{ab}. This means γ^a γ^b + γ^b γ^a = 2g^{ab}·I where I is the identity in spinor space. New struct GammaMatrix <: TensorExpr with spinor indices (suppressed) and one spacetime index. Implement gamma_chain_simplify that applies Clifford relation to reduce products. New file: src/fermions/gamma.jl. Ground truth: Wald GR Appendix B. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement GammaMatrix type with Clifford algebra contraction

description: Implement trace identities for gamma matrix chains. General rule: trace of odd number of gammas = 0. Implement gamma_trace(chain) that evaluates trace of gamma matrix product and returns a TensorExpr. Must integrate with existing simplify pipeline. description: Implement Fierz rearrangement identities for spinor bilinears. The 16 independent elements of the Clifford algebra form a basis: {I, γ^a, σ^{ab} = (i/2)[γ^a,γ^b], γ^a γ^5, γ^5}. Fierz identity: (ψ̄₁ Γ_A ψ₂)(ψ̄₃ Γ_B ψ₄) = Σ_CD C_{ABCD} (ψ̄₁ Γ_C ψ₄)(ψ̄₃ Γ_D ψ₂) where C_{ABCD} are known numerical coefficients (Fierz matrix). Implement fierz_rearrange(expr) that applies these identities. Ground truth: Peskin & Schroeder Eqs 3.76-3.79; Nishi, Am. J. Phys. 73, 1160 (2005). ~100 LOC.
title: Implement Fierz identities for spinor bilinears

description: Implement charge conjugation matrix C satisfying C γ^a C^{-1} = -(γ^a)^T. Properties: C^T = -C (antisymmetric), C^{-1} = C^T = -C. description: Implement the dual (epsilon-containing) invariant database for degrees 2-5. Dual invariants involve the Levi-Civita tensor: e.g., *R_{ab}^{cd}R_{cd}^{ab} (the Pontryagin density). description: Implement the differential invariant database for invariants with up to 2 covariant derivatives. These are scalars of the form nabla_a nabla_b R_{cdef}... contracted to a scalar. Key examples: Box R = nabla^a nabla_a R, nabla_a R_{bc} nabla^a R^{bc}, etc. In d=4 the independent count at (degree, n_derivs)=(1,2) is 2: Box R and |nabla Ric|^2 (with Ric^2 reducible via Bianchi). Data source: Garcia-Parrado & Martin-Garcia, Comp. Phys. Comm. 176 (2007) 246, Section 6. Add to src/invariants/database/differential_2.jl. ~100 LOC.
title: Invar: Differential invariant database up to 2 derivatives

Must handle: (a) derivative commutation generating lower-derivative + higher-degree algebraic terms, (b) integration-by-parts equivalences (for action-level applications). Data source: Garcia-Parrado & Martin-Garcia, Comp. Phys. Comm. 176 (2007) 246, Section 6. Add to src/invariants/database/differential_4.jl. ~100 LOC.
title: Invar: Differential invariant database up to 4 derivatives

description: Generate all possible full contractions of a tensor expression using a given metric. For a rank-2n tensor, enumerate all ways to pair 2n indices into n pairs, contract each pair with metric g^{ab}. Number of pairings = (2n-1)!! = (2n)!/(2^n n!). For example, rank-4 tensor T_{abcd}: contractions are g^{ab}g^{cd}T_{abcd}, g^{ac}g^{bd}T_{abcd}, g^{ad}g^{bc}T_{abcd} (3 = 3!! pairings). Must handle symmetries of the input tensor to avoid generating equivalent contractions. API: all_contractions(expr, metric_name; registry=reg) -> Vector{TensorExpr}. New file: src/algebra/all_contractions.jl. Ground truth: Nutma (2014) Sec 2.1. ~100 LOC.

description: Implement orthogonality integral relations for tensor harmonics on S^2. While algebraically correct, this decomposition is WRONG for kernel extraction because the cross term (1/2)tr(h)δ¹R has n_L=0,n_R=2 and its spin projection gives (0, 1.5) not the expected (1.25, -0.5). The CORRECT approach for the EH bilinear form is h^{ab}δ¹G_{ab} = h^{ab}(δ¹R_{ab} - (1/2)g_{ab}δ¹R), which gives the FP kernel directly. Verified: spin-2=2.5, spin-0s=-1.0.
title: HANDOFF documents wrong formula for EH bilinear: use h^{ab}δ¹G_{ab} not δ²R+cross

description: Implement Wald's covariant phase space formalism in TensorGR.jl. Extract equations of motion, symplectic potential/current, Noether current/charge from arbitrary Lagrangians. Validate with EH Noether charge (Komar integral) and black hole entropy (S=A/4). Ground truth: Margalef-Bentabol & Villaseñor, arXiv:2008.01842; Wald, J. Math. Phys. 31, 2378 (1990). New directory: src/phase_space/.
title: Covariant Phase Space (xCPS)

description:
Design Wald's covariant phase space formalism. Key objects and their relationships:
- Lagrangian d-form L[φ] (input)
- Equations of motion E: δL = E·δφ + dΘ
- Symplectic potential (d-1)-form Θ^μ[δφ] (boundary term from variation)
- Symplectic current (d-1)-form ω^μ[δ₁φ, δ₂φ] = δ₁Θ[δ₂φ] - δ₂Θ[δ₁φ]
- Noether current J^μ[ξ] = Θ^μ[£_ξφ] - ξ^μ L
- Noether charge Q^{μν}[ξ]: J = dQ (on-shell)

Integration with existing: variational_derivative, lie_derivative, exterior_d. New directory: src/phase_space/.

Ground truth: Wald, J. Math. Phys. 31, 2378 (1990); Margalef-Bentabol & Villaseñor, arXiv:2008.01842.

Acceptance: Design document with type hierarchy, data flow, API surface. Identify reusable components from existing codebase.
title: Research: Design covariant phase space framework for TensorGR.jl

description:
Validate Wald's black hole entropy formula for EH gravity. The entropy is:
  S = -2π ∮_H Q^{μν} ε_{μν}
where H is the bifurcation surface, ε_{μν} is the binormal, and Q is the Noether charge.

For L = R/(16πG): Q^{μν} = -(1/16πG)∇^{[μ} ξ^{ν]}. On the bifurcation surface with ∇^μ ξ^ν = κ ε^{μν}:
  S = (2π/16πG) · κ · A / κ = A/(4G)

Verify this symbolically using the xCPS pipeline.

Ground truth: Wald, PRL 70, 3684 (1993); Iyer & Wald, PRD 50, 846 (1994).

Acceptance: wald_entropy(L=R/(16piG), xi=horizon_generator) simplifies to A/(4G). For f(R) gravity, verify S = f'(R)·A/4.
title: xCPS Validation: Black hole entropy S = A/4 from Noether charge

description:
Implement extraction of equations of motion E from a Lagrangian L. Given L[φ, ∂φ, ∂²φ, ...], compute δL/δφ = 0 as tensor equations. Generalize existing variational_derivative to handle:
- Metric field: δL/δg_{μν} → G_{μν} for EH
- Matter fields: δL/δφ for scalars, δL/δA_μ for vectors
- Higher derivatives: integration by parts to isolate δφ

Key: separate E·δφ from total divergence dΘ. Add to src/phase_space/.

Ground truth: Wald (1990) Eq 2.1.

Acceptance: eom_extract(L, field) returns (E, Theta) where E is the EOM tensor and Theta is the symplectic potential boundary term. Verify: EH → Einstein tensor.
title: xCPS: Implement equations of motion extraction from Lagrangian

description:
Implement the symplectic potential (d-1)-form Θ^μ[δφ]. This is the boundary term from the variation of the Lagrangian: δL = E·δφ + ∇_μ Θ^μ. For EH gravity:
  Θ^μ[δg] = g^{αβ}(∇_μ δg_{αβ} - ∇_α δg_{μβ})

Θ is a (d-1)-form on spacetime valued in 1-forms on field space. Must track the field variation δφ as a separate tensor. Add to src/phase_space/.

Ground truth: Wald (1990) Eq 2.2; Iyer & Wald, PRD 50, 846 (1994) Eq 2.3.

Acceptance: symplectic_potential(L, field, delta_field) returns Θ^μ as TensorExpr. Verify for EH Lagrangian.
title: xCPS: Implement symplectic potential Θ

description:
Implement the symplectic current (d-1)-form ω^μ[δ₁φ, δ₂φ]. Definition:
  ω^μ[δ₁φ, δ₂φ] = δ₁Θ^μ[δ₂φ] - δ₂Θ^μ[δ₁φ]

This is the presymplectic form on field space. Key property: ∇_μ ω^μ = 0 when both δ₁φ and δ₂φ satisfy linearized EOM. The symplectic form Ω = ∫_Σ ω is independent of Cauchy surface Σ. Add to src/phase_space/.

Ground truth: Wald (1990) Eq 2.3.

Acceptance: symplectic_current(Theta, delta1_field, delta2_field) returns ω^μ as TensorExpr. Verify antisymmetry: ω[δ₁,δ₂] = -ω[δ₂,δ₁]. Verify conservation on-shell.
title: xCPS: Implement symplectic current ω

description:
Implement the Noether current (d-1)-form J^μ[ξ] associated with a diffeomorphism generated by vector field ξ:
  J^μ[ξ] = Θ^μ[£_ξ φ] - ξ^μ L

Key properties:
- ∇_μ J^μ = 0 when EOM satisfied (on-shell conservation)
- J^μ = E·£_ξφ + ∇_ν Q^{μν} (relation to Noether charge)

Uses existing lie_derivative infrastructure. Add to src/phase_space/.

Ground truth: Wald (1990) Eq 2.6.

Acceptance: noether_current(L, field, xi) returns J^μ as TensorExpr. Verify on-shell conservation ∇_μ J^μ = 0 for EH with Killing ξ.
title: xCPS: Implement Noether current J

description:
Implement the Noether charge (d-2)-form Q^{μν}[ξ] such that on-shell J^μ = ∇_ν Q^{μν}. For EH gravity:
  Q^{μν} = -∇^{[μ} ξ^{ν]}

The Noether charge is the integrand of conserved charges: H[ξ] = ∫_∂Σ Q. For Killing vectors, this gives Komar integrals. For horizons, this gives black hole entropy.

Requires potential extraction (finding Q from dQ = J). Add to src/phase_space/.

Ground truth: Wald (1990) Eq 2.8; Iyer & Wald, PRD 50, 846 (1994).

Acceptance: noether_charge(J, or equivalently L, field, xi) returns Q^{μν}. Verify for EH: Q = -∇^[μ ξ^ν].
title: xCPS: Implement Noether charge Q

description:
Implement divergence detection: given a tensor expression T^μ, determine if it can be written as ∇_ν S^{μν} for some S (i.e., is T^μ exact as a (d-1)-form?). This is essential for:
- Extracting Noether charge from Noether current
- Identifying boundary terms in variation
- Ambiguity analysis in symplectic potential

Algorithm: attempt integration by parts, check if remainder vanishes. Add to src/phase_space/.

Ground truth: xCPS documentation (Margalef-Bentabol & Villaseñor, arXiv:2008.01842).

Acceptance: is_divergence(expr) returns Bool. extract_potential(expr) returns S if divergence, nothing otherwise. Verify: ∇_ν ∇^[μ ξ^ν] is detected as divergence of ∇^[μ ξ^ν].
title: xCPS: Implement divergence detection utility

description:
Implement potential extraction: given ∇_a T^{abc...} = S^{bc...}, find T^{abc...}. This is the tensor analogue of finding an antiderivative. Key use case: given J^μ = ∇_ν Q^{μν}, extract Q^{μν} from J^μ.

Algorithm: For expressions involving covariant derivatives of known tensors, use algebraic identities and integration-by-parts to reconstruct the potential. Handle ambiguities (T + curl = T' also works). Add to src/phase_space/.

Ground truth: xCPS documentation (Margalef-Bentabol & Villaseñor, arXiv:2008.01842).

Acceptance: extract_potential(divergence_expr, div_index) returns the potential tensor. Verify round-trip: ∇_ν(extract_potential(J, ν)) simplifies to J.
title: xCPS: Implement potential extraction (antiderivative of divergence)

description:
Validate the covariant phase space pipeline for Einstein-Hilbert gravity L = R. For a Killing vector ξ, verify:
- Symplectic potential: Θ^μ = g^{αβ}(∇_μ δg_{αβ} - ∇_α δg_{μβ})
- Noether current: J^μ = 2G^{μν}ξ_ν + 2∇_ν∇^{[μ}ξ^{ν]}
- Noether charge: Q^{μν} = -∇^{[μ} ξ^{ν]} (the Komar integrand)
- Conserved quantity: H = -∮_{∂Σ} ∇^μ ξ^ν dS_{μν} (Komar integral)

Ground truth: Wald (1990) Sec 3; Komar, Phys. Rev. 113, 934 (1959).

Acceptance: End-to-end: L=R → Θ → J → Q. Q matches ∇^[μ ξ^ν] for EH. STRING MATCH.
title: xCPS Validation: EH Noether charge = Komar integral

description: Implement metric-affine gravity framework for TensorGR.jl, following xMAG (Helpin & Volkov, arXiv:2407.18019) and xBrauer. Independent metric g_{ab} and connection Gamma^a_{bc} (NOT Levi-Civita). Torsion T^a_{bc}, non-metricity Q_{abc}, distortion decomposition, metric-affine Riemann tensor, irreducible decomposition via Brauer algebra (11 pieces in d=4), Poincare gauge theory action. New directory: src/metric_affine/. Ground truth: Hehl, McCrea, Mielke, Ne'eman, Phys. Rep. 258, 1 (1995). ~1000 LOC total across 10 sub-issues.
title: Metric-Affine Gravity (xBrauer/xMAG)

description: Design how to represent independent metric g_{ab} and connection Gamma^a_{bc} (NOT Levi-Civita) in TensorGR.jl. Key challenge: existing covd infrastructure assumes metric compatibility. Decomposition: Gamma = Gamma_LC + N where Gamma_LC is Levi-Civita and N is distortion. Torsion: T^a_{bc} = Gamma^a_{[bc]} (antisymmetric part). Non-metricity: Q_{abc} = -nabla_a g_{bc} (failure of metric compatibility). Design decisions: (1) how to register a general connection (new covd type vs flag), (2) how torsion/non-metricity interact with existing curvature pipeline, (3) storage of irreducible decomposition data. Deliverable: design document. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 2. ~0 LOC (design only).
title: Research: Design metric-affine framework for TensorGR

description: Verify Einstein-Cartan theory: metric compatible (Q=0) with torsion. Tests: (1) Non-metricity vanishes: Q_{abc} = nabla_a g_{bc} = 0, (2) Distortion = contortion only: N^a_{bc} = K^a_{bc}, (3) Connection: Gamma^a_{bc} = Gamma_LC^a_{bc} + K^a_{bc}, (4) Field equations: G_{ab} = 8 pi G T_{ab} (Einstein) and T^a_{bc} + delta^a_b T^d_{dc} - delta^a_c T^d_{db} = 8 pi G S^a_{bc} (Cartan, where S is spin tensor), (5) Torsion is algebraically determined by spin (no propagating torsion). Verify that in vacuum (S=0), torsion vanishes and GR is recovered. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 4; Trautman, arXiv:gr-qc/0606062. ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: Einstein-Cartan theory (Q=0, T!=0)

description: Implement general affine connection Gamma^a_{bc} without metric compatibility or torsion-free assumptions. API: define_affine_connection!(reg, :Gamma; manifold=:M4, metric=:g) that registers a connection tensor with 3 indices (1 up, 2 down) and NO assumed symmetry in lower indices (unlike Levi-Civita which has Gamma^a_{(bc)}). The connection defines a covariant derivative: nabla_a V^b = partial_a V^b + Gamma^b_{ac} V^c. Must also register Levi-Civita part Gamma_LC and distortion N = Gamma - Gamma_LC as derived tensors. New file: src/metric_affine/connection.jl. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 2. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement general affine connection

description: Implement torsion tensor T^a_{bc} = 2 Gamma^a_{[bc]} = Gamma^a_{bc} - Gamma^a_{cb} (antisymmetric in lower indices). Irreducible decomposition (d=4): T^a_{bc} = (1/3)(T_b delta^a_c - T_c delta^a_b) + (1/3)epsilon^a_{bcd} S^d + q^a_{bc} where T_b = T^a_{ba} is the torsion vector trace, S^a = (1/6)epsilon^{abcd}T_{bcd} is the axial (pseudo-trace) vector, and q^a_{bc} is the tensor part (traceless, no axial part). Implement: (1) torsion_tensor(connection) -> TensorExpr, (2) torsion_vector(T), torsion_axial(T), torsion_tensor_part(T). New file: src/metric_affine/torsion.jl. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 2.3. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement torsion tensor with irreducible decomposition

description: Implement non-metricity tensor Q_{abc} = -nabla_a g_{bc} (symmetric in bc). Measures failure of metric compatibility. Irreducible decomposition (d=4): Q_{abc} = (1/4)g_{bc}Q_a + (1/d)g_{a(b}tilde_Q_{c)} + (2/(d-1)(d+2))(g_{a(b}Lambda_{c)} - g_{bc}Lambda_a) + Omega_{abc} where Q_a = g^{bc}Q_{abc} is the Weyl vector (trace), tilde_Q_a is the second trace, Lambda_a is mixed, and Omega_{abc} is the traceless tensor part. Implement: (1) nonmetricity_tensor(connection, metric) -> TensorExpr, (2) weyl_vector(Q), nonmetricity_traceless(Q). New file: src/metric_affine/nonmetricity.jl. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 2.4. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement non-metricity tensor with irreducible decomposition

description: Implement distortion tensor N^a_{bc} = Gamma^a_{bc} - Gamma_LC^a_{bc} decomposed as N^a_{bc} = K^a_{bc} + L^a_{bc} where K^a_{bc} is contortion (from torsion) and L^a_{bc} is disformation (from non-metricity). Contortion: K^a_{bc} = (1/2)(T^a_{bc} + T_{b}^{a}_{c} + T_{c}^{a}_{b}) with K_{abc} = -K_{bac}. Disformation: L^a_{bc} = (1/2)(Q^a_{bc} - Q_{b}^{a}_{c} - Q_{c}^{a}_{b}) with L_{abc} = L_{acb}. Key identity: N = K + L (complete decomposition). Implement: contortion(T), disformation(Q), distortion(connection). New file: src/metric_affine/distortion.jl. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Eq 2.8; Schouten 'Ricci Calculus'. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement distortion tensor decomposition

description: Implement Riemann tensor R^a_{bcd}(Gamma) for general affine connection Gamma. Definition: R^a_{bcd} = partial_c Gamma^a_{bd} - partial_d Gamma^a_{bc} + Gamma^a_{ce}Gamma^e_{bd} - Gamma^a_{de}Gamma^e_{bc}. Decompose into Riemannian part + torsion/non-metricity contributions: R^a_{bcd}(Gamma) = R^a_{bcd}(LC) + nabla_c N^a_{bd} - nabla_d N^a_{bc} + N^a_{ce}N^e_{bd} - N^a_{de}N^e_{bc}. Key difference from Riemannian case: R_{abcd} != -R_{abdc} still holds, but R_{abcd} != R_{cdab} in general (only with metric compatibility). Implement: ma_riemann(connection), ma_ricci(connection), ma_ricci_scalar(connection). New file: src/metric_affine/curvature.jl. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 3. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement metric-affine Riemann tensor

description: Implement irreducible decomposition of the metric-affine Riemann tensor R^a_{bcd}(Gamma) into 11 irreducible pieces in d=4, following the Brauer algebra approach of xBrauer. In metric-affine gravity, R_{abcd} has fewer symmetries than Riemannian R_{abcd}: only R_{ab[cd]} (antisymmetric in last pair). This gives 6*16 = 96 independent components (vs 20 for Riemannian). The 11 pieces correspond to irreducible representations of GL(4,R): 6 from the antisymmetric part (like Weyl, Ricci, scalar) and 5 from the non-symmetric part. Implement: brauer_decompose(R_ma) -> Dict{Symbol, TensorExpr} mapping piece names to projections. New file: src/metric_affine/brauer.jl. Ground truth: Helpin & Volkov, arXiv:2407.18019; McCrea, Class. Quant. Grav. 9, 553 (1992). ~100 LOC.
title: Implement irreducible decomposition via Brauer algebra (11 pieces)

description: Implement the most general Poincare gauge theory (PGT) action quadratic in curvature and torsion: L = -(1/2kappa)R + a_0 Lambda + Sigma_{i=1}^{3} a_i T^(i)_{abc} T^{(i)abc} + Sigma_{j=1}^{6} b_j R^(j)_{abcd} R^{(j)abcd} where T^(i) are the 3 irreducible torsion pieces and R^(j) are the 6 irreducible curvature pieces (from the parity-even sector). This gives 10 free coupling constants. Implement: pgt_lagrangian(params; connection=:Gamma, metric=:g) -> TensorExpr. Derive field equations by variation w.r.t. metric (Einstein-like) and connection (Cartan-like). New file: src/metric_affine/pgt_action.jl. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 5; Blagojevic 'Gravitation and Gauge Symmetries' Ch 5. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement Poincare gauge theory action

description: Verify that when torsion T=0 and non-metricity Q=0, the metric-affine framework reduces to standard GR. Tests: (1) Setting T^a_{bc}=0 and Q_{abc}=0 forces Gamma = Gamma_LC (Levi-Civita), (2) Distortion N=0, contortion K=0, disformation L=0, (3) R^a_{bcd}(Gamma) = R^a_{bcd}(LC) (standard Riemannian curvature), (4) PGT field equations reduce to Einstein equations G_{ab} + Lambda g_{ab} = 0, (5) All irreducible pieces of Brauer decomposition match standard Weyl/Ricci decomposition. STRING MATCH against existing TensorGR curvature results. New test file: test/test_metric_affine.jl. Ground truth: Hehl et al (1995) Sec 2.1. ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: Levi-Civita limit (T=0, Q=0 recovers GR)

description: Generalize the Horndeski Lagrangian from single scalar phi to N scalar fields phi^I (I=1..N) with multi-field kinetic matrix X^{IJ} = -1/2 nabla_a phi^I nabla^a phi^J. The Lagrangian becomes G_2(phi^I, X^{IJ}), G_3(phi^I, X^{IJ}) Box phi^I, etc. with internal field-space indices I,J contracted with a field-space metric G_{IJ}(phi). Must extend the existing Horndeski infrastructure to handle field-space index contractions alongside spacetime indices. ~100 LOC in src/scalar_tensor/multi_scalar.jl. Reference: Kobayashi, Rep. Prog. Phys. 82, 086901 (2019), arXiv:1901.04778, Sec 6; Ohashi et al, JCAP 1512 (2015) 009, arXiv:1507.04344. Test: N=1 reduction correctly recovers single-field Horndeski with G_2(phi, X), matching Kobayashi Sec 2.
title: Multi-scalar Horndeski: Generalize to N scalar fields with multi-field kinetic matrix

title: Invar Epic 6: Validation (Published Identity Tests)

description: Implement test verifying the Gauss-Bonnet identity: E_4 = R_{abcd}R^{abcd} - 4R_{ab}R^{ab} + R^2 is a total derivative in d=4. Using riemann_simplify with dim=4, the expression E_4 should simplify to zero (modulo total derivatives) or be recognized as the Euler density. This is the most fundamental DDI at degree 2. Test implementation: (1) construct E_4 as TensorExpr, (2) call riemann_simplify(E_4; dim=4), (3) verify result. Add to test/invariants/test_validation.jl. Ground truth: Lovelock, J. Austral. Math. Soc. 12 (1971) 385, Eq. (3.3); also Fulling et al (1992). String match: the simplified form should be zero or euler_density(:M4). ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: Gauss-Bonnet identity test in d=4

description: Implement test verifying exactly 3 independent quadratic Riemann invariants in d=4. The 3 independent invariants are: R^2, R_{ab}R^{ab}, and R_{abcd}R^{abcd} (Kretschner scalar). Test: (1) enumerate all distinct degree-2 Riemann monomials (there are 6 before cyclic/DDI reduction), (2) apply riemann_simplify to each, (3) verify they all reduce to linear combinations of the 3 independent invariants, (4) verify the 3 independent ones are genuinely independent (no relation among them). Ground truth: Fulling et al, Class. Quantum Grav. 9 (1992) 1151, Table 1. Add to test/invariants/test_validation.jl. ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: Degree-2 independence test (3 invariants in d=4)

description: Implement test verifying exactly 8 independent cubic Riemann invariants in d=4. The independent set includes: R^3, R*R_{ab}R^{ab}, R*R_{abcd}R^{abcd}, R_{a}^{b}R_{b}^{c}R_{c}^{a}, R_{ab}R_{cd}R^{acbd}, R_{ab}^{cd}R_{cd}^{ef}R_{ef}^{ab}, R_{a}^{bcd}R_{bce}^{f}R_{df}^{ae} (or equivalent bases), and one more. Test: (1) enumerate all distinct degree-3 monomials, (2) apply riemann_simplify to each, (3) verify all reduce to linear combinations of 8 independent ones, (4) verify no relation among the 8. Ground truth: Fulling et al, Class. Quantum Grav. 9 (1992) 1151, Table 2. Add to test/invariants/test_validation.jl. ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: Degree-3 independence test (8 invariants in d=4)

description: Implement test verifying the Weyl tensor completeness relation: C_{abcd}C^{abcd} = R_{abcd}R^{abcd} - 2R_{ab}R^{ab} + R^2/3 in d=4 (where d=4 is used for the 1/3 coefficient; in general d it is 2/((d-1)(d-2))). This is a standard identity relating the Weyl scalar squared to Kretschner, Ricci squared, and Ricci scalar squared. Test: (1) construct LHS - RHS, (2) call riemann_simplify, (3) verify result is zero. Also test via Weyl decomposition: weyl_to_riemann then simplify. Ground truth: standard GR identity, also Fulling et al (1992). Add to test/invariants/test_validation.jl. String match: simplified(LHS - RHS) == TScalar(0). ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: Weyl completeness identity test in d=4

description: Implement Dirac-Bergmann constrained Hamiltonian analysis for TensorGR.jl, following HiGGS (Barker, arXiv:2310.07484) and Hamilcar. Covers: ADM decomposition, Poisson brackets for tensor fields, primary/secondary constraint detection, first-class/second-class classification, DOF counting. Validates with GR (2 DOF) and Proca (3 DOF). New directory: src/hamiltonian/. Ground truth: Henneaux & Teitelboim 'Quantization of Gauge Systems' (1992); Arnowitt, Deser, Misner (1962). ~900 LOC total across 9 sub-issues.
title: Hamiltonian Analysis (HiGGS/Hamilcar)

description: Design how to implement canonical/Hamiltonian formalism in TensorGR.jl. Key concepts: (1) Phase space: canonical pairs (q_i, p^i) where q are configuration variables and p are conjugate momenta, (2) Legendre transform: p^i = dL/d(dq_i/dt), H = p^i q_dot^i - L, (3) Poisson brackets: {f,g} = df/dq dp/dp - df/dp dg/dq, (4) Constraints: relations phi(q,p) ~ 0 arising from singular Legendre transform (degenerate Hessian), (5) Classification: first-class (generate gauge transformations) vs second-class (physical constraints). Design decisions: (1) how to represent phase space variables in TensorGR AST, (2) how Poisson brackets act on tensor expressions, (3) how to detect/classify constraints algorithmically. Deliverable: design document. Ground truth: Henneaux & Teitelboim (1992) Ch 1. ~0 LOC (design only).
title: Research: Design Dirac-Bergmann constraint analysis for TensorGR

description: Implement ADM (Arnowitt-Deser-Misner) decomposition: g_{ab} -> (N, N^i, gamma_{ij}) where N is lapse, N^i is shift vector, gamma_{ij} is spatial metric on spacelike hypersurface. Line element: ds^2 = -N^2 dt^2 + gamma_{ij}(dx^i + N^i dt)(dx^j + N^j dt). Canonical momenta: pi^{ij} = (sqrt(gamma)/2N)(K^{ij} - gamma^{ij}K) where K_{ij} = (1/2N)(dot{gamma}_{ij} - D_i N_j - D_j N_i) is extrinsic curvature. ADM Hamiltonian: H = integral (N H + N^i H_i) where H = (pi^{ij}pi_{ij} - (1/2)pi^2)/sqrt(gamma) - sqrt(gamma)R^{(3)} (Hamiltonian constraint) and H_i = -2 D_j pi^j_i (momentum constraint). Build on existing foliation infrastructure. New file: src/hamiltonian/adm.jl. Ground truth: Arnowitt, Deser, Misner (1962); Wald Ch 10. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement ADM decomposition for Hamiltonian formulation

description: Implement Poisson bracket {f, g} for functionals of tensor fields on a spatial slice. For canonical pair (gamma_{ij}(x), pi^{kl}(y)): {gamma_{ij}(x), pi^{kl}(y)} = (1/2)(delta^k_i delta^l_j + delta^k_j delta^l_i) delta^3(x-y). General Poisson bracket: {F, G} = integral d^3x (delta F/delta gamma_{ij} delta G/delta pi^{ij} - delta F/delta pi^{ij} delta G/delta gamma_{ij}). Must handle: (1) functional derivatives of tensor expressions, (2) smeared constraints (integrated with test functions), (3) Poisson bracket algebra (Jacobi identity). API: poisson_bracket(F, G; canonical_pairs=[(gamma, pi)]). New file: src/hamiltonian/poisson.jl. Ground truth: Henneaux & Teitelboim Ch 1; Wald Ch 10. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement Poisson bracket for tensor fields

description: Implement detection of primary constraints from a Lagrangian. A primary constraint arises when the Legendre transform p^i = dL/d(q_dot^i) cannot be inverted for all velocities q_dot^i. This happens when the Hessian W_{ij} = d^2L/d(q_dot^i)d(q_dot^j) is singular (det W = 0). Algorithm: (1) Compute canonical momenta from Lagrangian, (2) Check if momenta definitions can be solved for velocities, (3) Relations that cannot be solved are primary constraints phi_alpha(q, p) ~ 0. For GR: pi_N ~ 0, pi_{N^i} ~ 0 (lapse and shift have no time derivatives in EH action). API: find_primary_constraints(lagrangian; variables=...). New file: src/hamiltonian/constraints.jl. Ground truth: Henneaux & Teitelboim Ch 1, Sec 1.1. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement primary constraint detection

description: Implement secondary constraint generation via consistency algorithm. For each primary constraint phi_alpha: require phi_dot_alpha = {phi_alpha, H_T} ~ 0 where H_T = H_canonical + lambda^alpha phi_alpha is the total Hamiltonian with Lagrange multipliers lambda. Three outcomes: (1) 0 ~ 0 (identity, no new info), (2) new constraint chi(q,p) ~ 0 (secondary constraint), (3) determines lambda^alpha (fixes a multiplier). Iterate: check consistency of secondary constraints too, until no new constraints arise. Algorithm terminates in finite steps. API: generate_secondary_constraints(primary_constraints, hamiltonian; canonical_pairs=...). Extends src/hamiltonian/constraints.jl. Ground truth: Henneaux & Teitelboim Ch 1, Sec 1.2. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement secondary constraint generation

description: Implement classification of constraints into first-class and second-class. Given all constraints {phi_alpha}: compute the constraint matrix C_{alpha beta} = {phi_alpha, phi_beta}|_{constraint surface}. First-class constraints: {phi_alpha, phi_beta} ~ 0 for ALL other constraints (weakly vanishing Poisson brackets). Generate gauge transformations. Second-class constraints: have at least one non-vanishing bracket. Physical constraints, reduce phase space. Algorithm: (1) Compute full constraint bracket matrix C, (2) Evaluate on constraint surface (set constraints ~ 0), (3) Find null vectors of C (first-class), (4) Remaining are second-class (must come in pairs for consistency). API: classify_constraints(all_constraints; canonical_pairs=...) -> (first_class, second_class). Extends src/hamiltonian/constraints.jl. Ground truth: Henneaux & Teitelboim Ch 1, Sec 1.3-1.4. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement constraint classification (first-class vs second-class)

description: Implement degree of freedom counting from constraint analysis. Formula: #DOF = #(canonical pairs) - #(first-class constraints) - (1/2)#(second-class constraints). This counts configuration-space DOF. Phase-space DOF = 2 * configuration DOF. Each first-class constraint removes 2 phase-space DOF (1 constraint + 1 gauge freedom). Each pair of second-class constraints removes 2 phase-space DOF. API: count_dof(canonical_pairs, first_class, second_class) -> (config_dof, phase_dof). Also implement: dof_summary(lagrangian; variables=...) that runs full pipeline (Legendre -> constraints -> classify -> count) and returns a summary. New file: src/hamiltonian/dof.jl. Ground truth: Henneaux & Teitelboim Sec 1.4; Barker (2023). ~100 LOC.
title: Implement DOF counting formula

description: Verify GR has 2 propagating DOF via Hamiltonian analysis. ADM variables: (N, N^i, gamma_{ij}) with conjugate momenta (pi_N, pi_{N^i}, pi^{ij}). Expected constraint structure: (1) Primary constraints: pi_N ~ 0 (1), pi_{N^i} ~ 0 (3) -> 4 primary, (2) Secondary constraints from consistency: H ~ 0 (Hamiltonian constraint, 1), H_i ~ 0 (momentum constraints, 3) -> 4 secondary, (3) All 8 constraints are first-class (generate diffeomorphisms), (4) No second-class constraints. DOF count: 10 canonical pairs (gamma_{ij}, pi^{ij}) + (N, pi_N) + (N^i, pi_{N^i}) - 8 first-class = 2 configuration DOF (graviton polarizations). Verify constraint algebra: {H(x), H(y)} = H_i delta'(x,y) etc. Ground truth: Arnowitt-Deser-Misner; Wald Ch 10; H&T Ch 4. New test file: test/test_hamiltonian.jl. ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: GR Hamiltonian (2 propagating DOF)

description: Verify massive vector (Proca) field has 3 propagating DOF via Hamiltonian analysis. Proca Lagrangian: L = -(1/4)F_{ab}F^{ab} + (1/2)m^2 A_a A^a where F_{ab} = partial_a A_b - partial_b A_a. Expected constraint structure: (1) Canonical momenta: pi^0 = 0 (primary constraint, A_0 has no time derivative), pi^i = F^{0i} = E^i (electric field), (2) Primary constraint: pi^0 ~ 0 (1 constraint), (3) Secondary constraint from consistency: {pi^0, H} = partial_i pi^i + m^2 A^0 ~ 0 (Gauss law, 1 constraint), (4) Both constraints are second-class (because m != 0 breaks gauge invariance): {pi^0, partial_i pi^i + m^2 A^0} = m^2 delta^3(x-y) != 0. DOF count: 4 canonical pairs - 0 first-class - (1/2)(2 second-class) = 3. In massless limit (m->0): constraints become first-class (gauge symmetry restored), DOF -> 2 (photon). Ground truth: H&T Sec 1.2; Greiter, Ann. Phys. 319, 217 (2005). ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: Proca Hamiltonian (3 propagating DOF)

description: End-to-end validation of the constraint enforcement system using the Weyl tensor. Setup: register Weyl C_{abcd} as (1) trace-free on all index pairs: C^a_{bad} = 0, (2) first Bianchi identity: C_{a[bcd]} = 0, (3) divergence-free in vacuum: nabla^a C_{abcd} = 0. Verify: (a) g^{ac} C_{abcd} automatically simplifies to zero (trace-free), (b) C_{abcd} + C_{acdb} + C_{adbc} automatically simplifies to zero (cyclic/Bianchi), (c) in vacuum spacetime, nabla^a C_{abcd} simplifies to zero (divergence-free). description: Implement bimetric gravity framework for TensorGR.jl, following bimEX (Torsello et al, arXiv:1904.10464). Two dynamical metrics g_{ab} and f_{ab} with Hassan-Rosen interaction potential. Covers: dual metric registration, interaction potential via elementary symmetric polynomials of matrix square root, bimetric field equations, ghost-freedom validation. New directory: src/bimetric/. Ground truth: Hassan & Rosen, JHEP 02 (2012) 126; Torsello et al (2019). ~600 LOC total across 6 sub-issues.
title: Bimetric Gravity (bimEX)

description: Design how to support two dynamical metrics g_{ab} and f_{ab} on the same manifold in TensorGR.jl. Key challenge: existing TensorGR registry supports one 'active' metric per manifold for contraction/simplification. Need multi-metric support. Design decisions: (1) how to register two metrics without conflict in metric_cache (separate namespaces?), (2) separate curvature tensors Riem_g, Riem_f, Ric_g, Ric_f etc., (3) how the matrix square root S = sqrt(g^{-1}f) is represented abstractly, (4) interaction with existing simplify pipeline (which metric contracts indices?). Note: existing product_manifold.jl already handles multi-metric in different factor spaces -- bimetric needs SAME manifold. Deliverable: design document. Ground truth: Hassan & Rosen (2012); Torsello et al (2019). ~0 LOC (design only).
title: Research: Design bimetric framework for TensorGR

description: Implement registration of two metrics g_{ab} and f_{ab} on the same manifold with independent curvature tensors. API: define_bimetric!(reg, :g, :f; manifold=:M4) that registers: (1) both metrics as symmetric rank-2 tensors, (2) separate Christoffel symbols Chris_g, Chris_f, (3) separate curvature tensors Riem_g, Ric_g, RicScalar_g, Ein_g and Riem_f, Ric_f, RicScalar_f, Ein_f, (4) separate covariant derivatives D_g, D_f. Must handle index raising/lowering with EITHER metric (context-dependent). Build on existing define_curvature_tensors! pattern. New file: src/bimetric/registration.jl. Ground truth: Hassan & Rosen (2012) Sec 2. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement dual metric registration with separate curvature tensors

description: Implement Hassan-Rosen bimetric interaction potential: V(g,f) = m^2 Sigma_{n=0}^{4} beta_n e_n(S) where S^a_b = (sqrt(g^{-1}f))^a_b is the matrix square root and e_n are elementary symmetric polynomials. Explicitly: e_0(S) = 1, e_1(S) = Tr(S) = S^a_a, e_2(S) = (1/2)((Tr S)^2 - Tr(S^2)), e_3(S) = (1/6)((Tr S)^3 - 3 Tr S Tr(S^2) + 2 Tr(S^3)), e_4(S) = det(S). The 5 parameters beta_0...beta_4 plus m^2 define the theory. Implement: hassan_rosen_potential(g, f, params) -> TensorExpr. New file: src/bimetric/potential.jl. Ground truth: Hassan & Rosen (2012) Eq 2.4; de Rham, Living Rev. Rel. 17, 7 (2014) Sec 8. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement Hassan-Rosen interaction potential

description: Implement abstract matrix square root S^a_b = (sqrt(g^{-1}f))^a_b as a tensor satisfying S^a_c S^c_b = g^{ac}f_{cb}. Key properties: (1) S is not symmetric in general (S_{ab} != S_{ba} w.r.t. either metric), (2) Defining relation: S^2 = g^{-1}f, (3) Symmetry: g_{ac}S^c_b = f_{ac}(S^{-1})^c_b (related to existence of square root). For perturbative expansion around proportional backgrounds (f = c^2 g): S = c(I + (1/2c^2)g^{-1}h_f - (1/2)g^{-1}h_g + ...). Implement: (1) matrix_sqrt_tensor(g, f) registers S with defining relation, (2) expand_matrix_sqrt(S, order) for perturbative expansion. New file: src/bimetric/matrix_sqrt.jl. Ground truth: Hassan & Rosen (2012) Sec 2; Torsello et al (2019) Sec 3. ~100 LOC.
title: Implement abstract matrix square root S = sqrt(g^{-1}f)

description: Implement bimetric field equations from varying the Hassan-Rosen action S = M_g^2 integral R(g) sqrt(g) + M_f^2 integral R(f) sqrt(f) + 2m^2 integral V(g,f) sqrt(g). The g-equation: G^g_{ab} + (m^2/M_g^2) V_{ab}^g = 0 where V_{ab}^g = delta(sqrt(g)V)/delta(g^{ab})/sqrt(g). The f-equation: G^f_{ab} + (m^2/M_f^2) V_{ab}^f = 0. The variation of V involves: V_{ab}^g = Sigma_{n=0}^{3} (-1)^n beta_n g_{a(c} Y^{(n)c}_{b)} where Y^{(n)} are matrix functions of S. Implement: bimetric_equations(g, f, params) -> (g_eqn, f_eqn). Also implement Bianchi constraint: nabla^g_a V^g_{ab} = 0 (follows from diffeomorphism invariance). New file: src/bimetric/equations.jl. Ground truth: Hassan & Rosen (2012) Eqs 2.6-2.7; Schmidt-May & von Strauss, J. Phys. A 49, 183001 (2016). ~100 LOC.
title: Implement bimetric field equations

description: Verify ghost-freedom of Hassan-Rosen bimetric theory: 7 propagating DOF (5 massive spin-2 + 2 massless spin-2), NOT 6+1 (which would include Boulware-Deser ghost). Tests at linearized level around proportional backgrounds f=c^2 g: (1) Expand bimetric action to quadratic order in perturbations h_g, h_f, (2) Diagonalize into mass eigenstates: massless mode h_0 and massive mode h_m with Fierz-Pauli mass term, (3) Verify Fierz-Pauli structure: m^2(h_{ab}h^{ab} - h^2) (relative sign is crucial for ghost freedom), (4) Count DOF: massless spin-2 = 2, massive spin-2 = 5, total = 7, (5) Verify absence of scalar ghost mode (Boulware-Deser). Ground truth: Hassan & Rosen (2012) Sec 3; Boulware & Deser, Phys. Rev. D 6, 3368 (1972). New test file: test/test_bimetric.jl. ~100 LOC.
title: Validation: Boulware-Deser ghost freedom (7 DOF)

description: Implement the GHP (Geroch-Held-Penrose) spin and boost weight system. Every GHP quantity has a pair of weights (p,q) under the rescaling l->lambda*l, n->lambda^{-1}*n, m->e^{i*theta}*m. A quantity eta has type {p,q} if eta -> lambda^{(p+q)/2} e^{i*(p-q)/2 theta} eta. Examples: Psi_0 has type {4,0}, rho has type {1,1}, sigma has type {3,-1}. Implement as metadata/trait on NP scalars. Struct: GHPWeight(p::Int, q::Int). Function: ghp_weight(scalar) -> GHPWeight. ~60 LOC in src/spinors/ghp.jl. Reference: GHP (1973) Section 2. Test: (1) verify weights of all 12 spin coefficients match Table 1 of GHP, (2) Psi_0 weight = {4,0}, Psi_4 weight = {-4,0}, (3) product rule: weight of product = sum of weights.
title: GHP: Implement spin/boost weight system

description: Implement the Ashtekar-Barbero connection A^i_a = Gamma^i_a + gamma K^i_a and the densitized triad E^a_i = sqrt(det(q)) e^a_i, where Gamma is the spin connection compatible with the triad, K^i_a = K_{ab} e^{bi} is the extrinsic curvature in triad form, gamma is the Barbero-Immirzi parameter, and e^a_i is the triad. These are the fundamental canonical variables of loop quantum gravity: {A^i_a(x), E^b_j(y)} = 8 pi G gamma delta^i_j delta^b_a delta(x,y). Must compute curvature F^i_{ab} of A and express the Gauss, vector, and scalar constraints. ~120 LOC in src/spinors/ashtekar_variables.jl. Reference: Ashtekar, Lectures on Non-Perturbative Canonical Gravity (1991), Ch 10, Eq 10.1.5-10.1.12; Barbero, PRD 51, 5507 (1995). Test: STRING MATCH — F^i_{ab} of A^i_a expanded in terms of Gamma and K gives F = R + gamma^2 K wedge K + gamma DK, reproducing Ashtekar (1991) Eq 10.1.10 (curvature decomposition).
title: SpaceSpinors: Implement Ashtekar-Barbero connection and densitized triad variables

description: Implement representation of 1-loop Feynman integrals arising from graviton exchange diagrams. Express as standard momentum integrals: int d^d q / (2pi)^d [numerator(q, k)] / [q^2 (q-k)^2 ...]. Include d-dimensional regularization stubs: track spacetime dimension d symbolically, implement dim-reg identity int d^d q q^a q^b / (q^2)^n = (eta^{ab}/d) int d^d q q^2 / (q^2)^n. Store integrals as symbolic objects with Passarino-Veltman reduction stubs. ~100 LOC in src/feynman/loop_integrals.jl. Reference: Goldberger & Rothstein, PRD 73, 104029 (2006), arXiv:hep-th/0409156, Sec 5; Leibbrandt, Rev. Mod. Phys. 47, 849 (1975). Test: box integral topology identification — a 2-loop diagram with 4 propagators is correctly classified as box integral I_4.
title: EFTofPNG: Implement loop integral representation with d-dimensional regularization

description: Implement DDI generation from xTras. Key identity: δ^{[a1...a_{d+1}]}_{[b1...b_{d+1}]} = 0 in dimension d generates identities between Riemann monomials. Ground truth: Lovelock, J. Austral. Math. Soc. 12 (1971) 385; Nutma Sec 3. 6 sub-issues, ~100 LOC each.
title: Dimensionally-Dependent Identities (DDIs) (xTras Gap)

description: Design how to implement ConstructDDIs (dimensionally-dependent identities). Key identity: the totally antisymmetrized generalized Kronecker delta δ^{[a1...a_{d+1}]}_{[b1...b_{d+1}]} = 0 in dimension d, because you cannot antisymmetrize d+1 indices in d dimensions. Strategy: (1) write δ^{a1...a_{d+1}}_{b1...b_{d+1}} = 0 as determinant, (2) expand and contract with curvature tensors to generate identities between Riemann monomials, (3) at each derivative order, enumerate independent DDIs. Key questions: what data structure for DDIs? How to integrate with simplify pipeline? How to enumerate DDIs systematically at a given curvature order? Ground truth: Lovelock, J. Austral. Math. Soc. 12 (1971) 385; Harvey (1995). Output: design notes, not code.
title: Research: DDI generation strategy

description: Implement the generalized Kronecker delta δ^{a1...ak}_{b1...bk} = det of the k x k matrix (δ^{ai}_{bj}). This is the totally antisymmetrized product of ordinary Kronecker deltas. For k > d (manifold dimension), it vanishes identically -- this is the source of DDIs. API: generalized_kronecker_delta(k, up_indices, down_indices; registry=reg) -> TensorExpr. Should expand as sum of signed products of ordinary deltas. Also: is_zero_by_dimension(expr) to detect vanishing by antisymmetry. Ground truth: Lovelock (1971). ~100 LOC in src/algebra/ddi.jl.
title: Implement generalized Kronecker delta

description: Generate DDIs from δ^{a1...a5}_{b1...b5} = 0 in d=4, contracted with rank-2 tensors (Ricci, metric). These produce identities between products of Ricci/metric tensors. Example: contracting δ^5 = 0 with two Ricci tensors gives relations like R_{ab}R^{ab} in terms of R^2 and Rab products. Algorithm: (1) start with δ^{d+1} = 0, (2) contract pairs of indices with R_{ab} or g_{ab}, (3) expand using generalized_kronecker_delta, (4) simplify to get identity. Should enumerate all independent DDIs at a given tensor product order. Ground truth: Harvey (1995); Nutma Sec 3. ~100 LOC in src/algebra/ddi.jl.
title: Implement DDI generation for rank-2 tensors

description: Generate DDIs from δ^{d+1} = 0 contracted with Riemann tensor (rank-4). These produce the most physically important identities, e.g. the relation between Kretschner, RabRab, and R^2 in d=4. Contract δ^{a1...a5}_{b1...b5} = 0 with R^{b1b2}_{a1a2}: gives identity involving R_{abcd}R^{abcd}, R_{ab}R^{ab}, R^2. Higher-order: contract with two Riemanns for cubic DDIs. API: generate_ddis(order; dim, registry) -> Vector{TensorExpr} where each expr = 0. Ground truth: Lovelock (1971). ~100 LOC in src/algebra/ddi.jl.
title: Implement DDI generation for Riemann tensor

description: Integrate DDI reduction into simplify() pipeline when manifold dimension is known. After standard simplification (canonicalize + contract + Bianchi), apply DDIs to reduce expression to a minimal basis of independent invariants. Strategy: (1) detect curvature monomials in expression, (2) look up applicable DDIs for that dimension, (3) use DDIs as rewrite rules to eliminate dependent terms, (4) choose canonical basis (e.g., prefer Kretschner over R_{abcd}R^{acbd}). API: simplify(expr; use_ddis=true, registry=reg) or separate ddi_reduce(expr; registry=reg). Ground truth: Nutma Sec 3. ~100 LOC in src/algebra/ddi.jl.
title: Implement DDI simplification pass in simplify pipeline

description: Validate DDI infrastructure with the Lanczos-Lovelock identity. In d=4: δ^{abcde}_{fghij} R^{fg}_{ab} R^{hi}_{cd} = 0 implies the Gauss-Bonnet combination G = R_{abcd}R^{abcd} - 4R_{ab}R^{ab} + R^2 is a total derivative (topological). Test: (1) construct δ^5 contracted with two Riemanns, (2) expand using generalized_kronecker_delta, (3) simplify, (4) verify result is proportional to Gauss-Bonnet density, (5) cross-check against existing euler_density(:M4) in src/gr/topological.jl. Ground truth: Lovelock (1971); Lanczos (1938). Test file: test/test_ddi.jl.
title: Validation: Lanczos-Lovelock identity in d=4

description: Implement index-free tensor notation from xTras. Represent tensor expressions without explicit indices (e.g., Ric·Ric for R_{ab}R^{ab}). Options: overload · operator or create IndexFree wrapper type. Ground truth: Nutma Sec 5. 4 sub-issues, ~100 LOC each.
title: Index-Free Notation (xTras Gap)

description: Design index-free tensor notation for TensorGR.jl. Options: (1) overload Julia dot operator (LinearAlgebra.dot or custom) for contractions like Ric . Ric meaning R_{ab}R^{ab}, (2) create IndexFree wrapper type that stores contraction pattern without explicit index names, (3) operator overloading with * for tensor product and | for contraction. Key design questions: (a) how to represent partial contractions (some indices contracted, some free), (b) how to handle multiple possible contraction patterns (R_{abcd}R^{abcd} vs R_{abcd}R^{acbd}), (c) syntax for traces, (d) conversion to/from indexed form. Ground truth: Nutma Sec 5. Output: design notes, not code.
title: Research: Design index-free notation

description: Implement IndexFree wrapper type that stores a tensor expression in index-free form with contraction pattern. Fields: tensors::Vector{Symbol} (tensor names in order), contraction_pattern::Vector{Tuple{Int,Int,Int,Int}} (pairs of (tensor_idx, slot_idx, tensor_idx, slot_idx) for each contraction), free_slots::Vector{Tuple{Int,Int}} (uncontracted slots). Example: IndexFree([:Ric, :Ric], [(1,1,2,2), (1,2,2,1)], []) represents R_{ab}R^{ba} = R_{ab}R^{ab}. Include pretty-printing: show as Ric . Ric or similar. ~100 LOC in src/algebra/index_free.jl.
title: Implement IndexFree type

description: Convert from indexed TensorExpr to IndexFree form. Algorithm: (1) identify all dummy index pairs (contracted indices), (2) record which tensor slots they connect, (3) identify free indices, (4) build IndexFree with contraction pattern. Must handle: TProduct (multiple tensors), metric contractions (g^{ab} as identity contraction), covariant derivatives (treated as additional tensor slots). API: to_index_free(expr; registry=reg) -> IndexFree. Should canonicalize the contraction pattern (sort pairs) for unique representation. ~100 LOC in src/algebra/index_free.jl.
title: Implement ToIndexFree conversion

description: Convert from IndexFree back to indexed TensorExpr with auto-generated dummy index names. Algorithm: (1) assign fresh dummy names from a canonical sequence (e.g., :d1, :d2, ...) for each contraction pair, (2) assign free index names from user specification or canonical sequence (:a, :b, ...), (3) build TProduct with concrete TIndex entries, (4) wrap in appropriate up/down positions based on tensor properties from registry. API: from_index_free(ifree; free_names=[:a,:b], registry=reg) -> TensorExpr. Round-trip: from_index_free(to_index_free(expr)) should simplify to expr. ~100 LOC in src/algebra/index_free.jl.
title: Implement FromIndexFree conversion

description: Implement the 5 complex Weyl scalars as projections of the Weyl tensor onto the null tetrad: Psi_0 = C_{abcd} l^a m^b l^c m^d, Psi_1 = C_{abcd} l^a n^b l^c m^d, Psi_2 = C_{abcd} l^a m^b mbar^c n^d, Psi_3 = C_{abcd} l^a n^b mbar^c n^d, Psi_4 = C_{abcd} n^a mbar^b n^c mbar^d. Equivalently, components of the Weyl spinor: Psi_0 = Psi_{ABCD} o^A o^B o^C o^D, etc. where o^A, iota^A form a spin frame. Register as named NP scalars. Function: compute_weyl_scalars(tetrad; registry). ~80 LOC in src/spinors/np.jl. Reference: NP (1962) Eq 4.3. Test: (1) string-match each Psi_n definition, (2) Schwarzschild: only Psi_2 nonzero.
title: NP: Implement 5 Weyl scalars Psi_0...Psi_4
